prose:
**Fukui reactivity indices for phenol**

Method provenance and calculation details:
Property: condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), dual descriptor f⁰, and identification of the most electrophilic/nucleophilic atoms.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`) with exact four-center integrals (density fitting OFF) in PySCF 2.x.
Geometry provenance: fixed experimental‐like geometry supplied by the user (`phenol.xyz`). No further optimisation was performed; the Fukui analysis was a single-point calculation on this coordinate set (DFT//user geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1). The N–1 and N+1 states were treated as doublets (charge ±1, multiplicity 2) in the finite-difference scheme.
Software: chemkit `fukui` skill (backend PySCF) — see full CLI in the JSON (`cli_invocation`).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles converged for all three charge states (`integrity.status = ok`, `trustworthy = true`).  Integrity checks note charge-conservation diagnostics:
• “sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)”  
• “sum(f−) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)”  
Both checks passed (`ok: true`).  No other warnings were reported.
Live log: calculation wrote a live PySCF log to `phenol_fukui.out` (path surfaced while running); final structured result JSON is `phenol_fukui_dft.json`.

Headline results (atom indices are 1-based as in the output):
Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺): atom 3 (C)  f⁺ = 2.1028319  
Most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻): atom 1 (O)  f⁻ = 0.16201477

Selected condensed Fukui functions (unitless):
Atom 1 O  f⁺ = −0.99513677   f⁻ = 0.16201477   f⁰ = −0.416561  
Atom 3 C  f⁺ =  2.1028319    f⁻ = 0.070972379  f⁰ =  1.0869021  
(See `phenol_fukui_dft.json` for the complete 13-atom list.)

The reported headline number in this summary is the largest electrophilic Fukui index (f⁺) returned by the tool:
2.1028319

Integrity verdict: trustworthy = true.
Provenance: computed (DFT calculation performed as described above).
